meta.title: Community Leaders & Advocacy | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Community engagement plus creative advocacy and storytelling to help participants build voice and belonging.

hero.title: Community Leadership & Self-Advocacy
hero.subtitle: Community outings + creative advocacy and storytelling—helping participants show up, speak up, and be seen.
hero.bestFor: Self-advocates and anyone who wants to be more active in their community.

blocks.html: This program helps participants show up, speak up, and be seen in their community. It combines community outings with creative advocacy and storytelling.

items: Local events, fairs, concerts, art shows
Library visits, workshops, clubs, or meetups
Helping participants tell their story in their own way (video, audio, writing, art)
Creating positive social media or digital content (with family approval)
Practicing speaking up about their needs and preferences
Meeting community partners (coaches, artists, business owners)
Finding places they want to return to regularly
Real connections with local people and organizations

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.prompt: Want help identifying go-to places and community connections?

blocks.html: Scheduled as community-based sessions where a staff member accompanies the participants to events and/or helps them create content afterward.

Supports funded through DDD Goods & Services focus on helping the person engage in community activities they care about and express themselves through advocacy. This may include attending events with support, practicing social skills, and working on simple advocacy or storytelling projects (video, writing, or art) that reflect the person's interests and goals.
